cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the movies. The problem gives us some information about Leslie's schedule and where different people saw them. Let me break down each part step by step. First, Leslie woke up at 6am. Then Susan saw Leslie on a rooftop from 6am to 7am. So that's the first time window: 6 to 7 am. Then Mary saw Leslie working at the office from 12pm to 2pm. So that's noon to 2 pm. Steven saw Leslie getting coffee at the cafe from 2pm to 7pm. So that's from 2 pm until 7 pm. The movies closed after 7 pm, so Leslie had to go before that. Now, we need to find the time when Leslie wasn't accounted for. Let's list out the times Leslie was seen: 1. 6am - 7am: on the rooftop (Susan) 2. 12pm - 2pm: at the office (Mary) 3. 2pm - 7pm: at the cafe (Steven) The movies closed after 7pm, so Leslie couldn't go after that. So the possible times are the gaps between these intervals. From when Leslie woke up at 6am until 7am, they were on the rooftop. Then from 7am to 12pm, what was Leslie doing? The problem doesn't mention anything else. So between 7 am and 12 pm, Leslie isn't accounted for. Then from 12 to 2 pm, they were at the office. From 2 pm to 7 pm, they were at the cafe. After 7 pm, the movies are closed. So the only time when Leslie's whereabouts aren't specified is between 7 am and 12 pm. That's when they could have gone to the movies. Let me check the options: A) 7am to 12pm B) 2pm to 7pm C) 12pm
